customers.name: Growth Commerce GM
customers.description: A digital commerce leader responsible for conversion, authorization performance, global expansion, and reliable money operations across web, mobile, and increasingly in-person channels. Success means capturing more legitimate demand without growing fraud, support burden, or finance complexity.
careAbout: Maximizing successful payment conversion on every checkout session
Adding local payment methods without long integration cycles
Reducing authorization failures caused by issuer, SCA, or orchestration gaps
Keeping refunds, disputes, and reconciliation manageable as volume scales
Using one platform across online, mobile, and in-person commerce
winsThem: Hosted and embeddable checkout surfaces that ship quickly
Strong authorization optimization and payment-method coverage
Unified reporting across charges, refunds, disputes, and payouts
Built-in fraud controls that protect conversion rather than only block volume
A clear path from online payments into Terminal and finance operations
theirFear: Losing revenue because checkout conversion degrades silently by market or payment method
Adding operational overhead every time they launch a new country or channel
Creating finance, support, or fraud problems while chasing growth

jobsToBeDone.name: Accept more payments globally with higher conversion
jobsToBeDone.what_it_is: Turn purchase intent into successfully authorized and captured payments across markets, devices, and payment methods with minimal customer friction.
jobsToBeDone.outcome: Higher captured revenue from the same demand while keeping fraud and operational cost controlled.

steps.step: Launch a checkout flow that buyers trust
steps.description: Stand up a payment experience that works fast on web and mobile and supports saved details, wallets, and local methods.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Hosted Checkout
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Provides a fast-to-launch, conversion-optimized checkout surface with built-in payment UX.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Payment Elements
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Lets teams embed a customizable payment form while keeping method logic centralized.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Payment Method Orchestration
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Shows the right payment methods by market, device, and customer context.

steps.step: Improve payment success on legitimate demand
steps.description: Reduce payment failure caused by issuer declines, authentication friction, and low-quality routing.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Authorization Optimization
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Uses retries, network tokens, and routing improvements to recover legitimate transactions.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Fraud Detection and Adaptive Risk Controls
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Blocks abusive behavior while preserving approval rates on good customers.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Disputes and Chargeback Operations
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Keeps post-payment loss and support cost from eroding conversion gains.

steps.description: Understand balances, refunds, reconciliation, and payout timing well enough to scale internationally.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Balances and Internal Ledger
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Tracks money movement states across authorizations, captures, refunds, and payouts.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Reconciliation and Balance Operations
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Explains how payment events map into balances and cash movement.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Reporting, Sigma, and Data Export
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Gives finance and operations teams usable transaction-level reporting.

jobsToBeDone.name: Unify online and in-person commerce without fragmenting operations
jobsToBeDone.what_it_is: Add in-person payment acceptance and omnichannel refund or reporting workflows without creating a second payments stack.
jobsToBeDone.outcome: One commerce platform across online, mobile, and physical touchpoints.

steps.step: Extend the same payment platform into stores or field operations
steps.description: Bring card-present payments into the operating model without rebuilding finance processes.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Terminal and Reader Operations
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Adds in-person payment acceptance and reader lifecycle management.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Payment Method Orchestration
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Keeps payment-method handling consistent across channels where relevant.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Balances and Internal Ledger
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Keeps online and in-person payment states legible in one money model.

steps.step: Give support and finance one source of truth
steps.description: Handle refunds, disputes, and reporting centrally even when channels differ.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Disputes and Chargeback Operations
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Centralizes post-transaction issue handling across payment channels.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Reconciliation and Balance Operations
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Keeps cash-flow, payout, and exception workflows aligned.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Eventing and Webhooks
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Pushes consistent payment events into order, support, and ERP systems.

customerJourneyStories.name: Global checkout expansion without conversion loss
customerJourneyStories.summary: A commerce GM wants to add countries, payment methods, and physical touchpoints while protecting conversion and keeping finance operations understandable.

stages.narrative: Growth slows in existing checkout flows or new markets require more localized payment support.
The GM looks for a platform that reduces implementation effort while still improving authorization and conversion.
They compare checkout UX, payment-method breadth, fraud controls, reporting quality, and whether in-person support fits the same operating model.
They launch a new checkout or payment-method cohort and watch authorization, abandonment, and support exceptions closely.
The real test is whether payment success improves without creating dispute, reconciliation, or payout confusion.
Stripe remains the preferred platform when new markets and channels can be added faster than internal operations complexity grows.

top.name: Successful payment conversion
top.description: Increase the share of legitimate checkout attempts that become captured payments to 97.5%.

children.name: Checkout completion rate
children.description: Increase completed payment-form submissions on initiated checkout sessions to 84%.

children.name: Local payment method coverage
children.description: Reach 92% coverage of target-market preferred payment methods across active expansion countries.

children.name: Issuer authorization rate
children.description: Raise issuer authorization success on legitimate card attempts to 91.5%.

children.name: Fraud-adjusted dispute loss rate
children.description: Keep dispute and confirmed fraud losses below 0.18% of processed volume.

top.name: Enterprise merchant GPV retention
top.description: Keep net GPV retention above 118% on scaled commerce cohorts by improving conversion and cross-product depth.

children.name: Link-enabled returning checkout share
children.description: Grow the share of returning checkouts using saved payment details and accelerated completion to 36%.

children.name: Terminal attach on omnichannel accounts
children.description: Reach 22% Terminal attach among target omnichannel commerce accounts.

children.name: Reconciliation exception rate
children.description: Reduce merchant-reported payout and balance exceptions to fewer than 0.35% of payout cycles.

children.name: Manual fraud review rate
children.description: Keep manual fraud review under 3.2% of screened payment attempts.

productStrategy.vision: Make Stripe the default growth engine for digital commerce teams by compounding checkout conversion, payment success, and money-operations clarity.

1_year.focus: Improve payment conversion, issuer performance, and omnichannel operational clarity.

supporting: Terminal attach on omnichannel accounts to 22%

strategicMilestones: Hosted and embedded checkout flows become easier to localize by market
Authorization optimization becomes more visible and actionable to merchant operators
Online and in-person reporting align into one operational picture

3_year.focus: Turn Stripe into the operating layer for global commerce conversion and payment operations.

customerKPI.northStar: Higher captured revenue from the same demand across markets and channels
supporting: Lower support burden per payment exception

businessKPI.northStar: Higher merchant lifetime value through deeper product adoption
supporting: Lower operational churn in scaled merchant cohorts

strategicMilestones: Stripe becomes the fastest path to launch and optimize international checkout
Commerce operators treat payment acceptance, fraud, and reconciliation as one system
Unified commerce data becomes a decision surface for growth and finance teams

5_year.focus: Make Stripe the trusted infrastructure layer for every serious commerce business moving money across channels.

customerKPI.northStar: Merchants rely on Stripe as the default path for new channel and market expansion
supporting: Higher share of commerce volume consolidated on Stripe

businessKPI.northStar: Durable payments-led expansion into adjacent commerce and finance workflows
supporting: Higher platform share in omnichannel enterprise accounts

strategicMilestones: Stripe becomes the most programmable and operationally legible commerce payment stack
Fraud, payment success, and finance automation compound into a defensible moat
Merchants choose Stripe because expansion complexity stays lower for longer

customers.name: SaaS Revenue Operations Lead
customers.description: A revenue operations or monetization leader responsible for subscription growth, usage-based packaging, failed-payment recovery, invoicing, tax, and month-end accuracy. Success means shipping pricing changes faster while reducing involuntary churn and manual finance work.
careAbout: Launching recurring and usage-based pricing without heavy engineering involvement
Recovering failed recurring payments before they become churn
Closing invoices and collections predictably across geographies
Handling tax and revenue recognition correctly as the business scales
Giving finance, product, and GTM teams one monetization source of truth
winsThem: Flexible product, price, and meter models
Subscription dunning and retry logic that actually recovers revenue
Invoice, tax, and revenue-recognition workflows tied to the same data model
Clear reporting for ARR, collections, and churn diagnostics
A credible path from startup billing to enterprise-grade finance operations
theirFear: Shipping monetization changes slowly because billing logic is brittle
Losing healthy customers to involuntary churn or invoice friction
Creating a finance close process that depends on manual spreadsheets and exceptions

jobsToBeDone.name: Launch recurring and usage-based monetization quickly
jobsToBeDone.what_it_is: Turn packaging, pricing, and contract changes into live subscription or invoice flows with low engineering dependency.
jobsToBeDone.outcome: Faster monetization experiments and smoother expansion revenue.

steps.description: Configure recurring, hybrid, or usage-based plans without rebuilding core billing logic.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Subscription and Price Management
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Defines products, prices, proration, trials, and contract lifecycle behavior.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Usage Metering and Rating
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Turns product usage into billable records and rated amounts.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Invoicing and Collections
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Creates payable invoices and collection workflows from recurring and usage events.

steps.step: Collect recurring revenue without avoidable churn
steps.description: Recover failed payments and keep subscription status transitions understandable to customers and support teams.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Authorization Optimization
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Improves renewal success on legitimate payment attempts.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Invoicing and Collections
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Runs reminders, retries, and collections behavior across invoice states.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Eventing and Webhooks
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Synchronizes subscription state into product access and internal systems.

steps.step: Close the loop with finance and compliance
steps.description: Make tax, revenue recognition, and reporting robust enough for board and audit scrutiny.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Tax Determination and Registration
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Calculates tax and monitors registration obligations as monetization expands.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Revenue Recognition
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Transforms billing events into auditable accounting treatment.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Reporting, Sigma, and Data Export
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Gives RevOps and finance consistent ARR, churn, and collections reporting.

jobsToBeDone.name: Reduce month-end manual work while keeping revenue data trustworthy
jobsToBeDone.what_it_is: Create a billing and finance operating model where invoices, payments, taxes, and accounting outputs stay aligned.
jobsToBeDone.outcome: Faster close with fewer manual adjustments and fewer trust gaps between GTM and finance.

steps.step: Keep billing events and cash states aligned
steps.description: Understand how invoices, retries, payments, credits, and refunds affect receivables and balances.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Balances and Internal Ledger
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Provides the money-state backbone behind invoice collection and refunds.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Reconciliation and Balance Operations
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Explains settlement, payout, and exception handling to finance teams.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Reporting, Sigma, and Data Export
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Produces queryable transaction and billing detail for close workflows.

steps.description: Make tax and revenue outputs audit-friendly as the pricing model gets more complex.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Tax Determination and Registration
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Applies the right tax treatment and registration awareness to billed activity.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Revenue Recognition
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Automates revenue schedules and deferred revenue treatment.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Developer API and Dashboard Foundations
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Exposes configurable controls and audit trails for operators.

customerJourneyStories.name: Usage-based monetization without finance breakage
customerJourneyStories.summary: A RevOps lead wants to add usage pricing and invoice complexity without sacrificing collection rates, tax correctness, or month-end trust.

stages.narrative: The business needs new packaging, international expansion, or enterprise billing terms that outgrow a simple subscription setup.
The RevOps lead looks for billing infrastructure that can launch quickly without forcing custom finance tooling.
They compare pricing flexibility, dunning quality, invoicing, tax support, reporting, and revenue-recognition depth.
They launch new prices or meters in a limited cohort and watch failed-payment recovery and invoice exceptions.
The relationship deepens when billing, collections, tax, and accounting can all run from one event model.
Stripe stays embedded when monetization can keep evolving without causing recurring month-end cleanup projects.

top.name: Net revenue retention
top.description: Increase net revenue retention on the recurring cohort to 112% by reducing involuntary churn and improving monetization agility.

children.name: Recovered failed recurring revenue
children.description: Recover 56% of failed recurring payment value before it becomes churn.

children.name: Invoice collection within 30 days
children.description: Collect 95% of billed invoice value within 30 days of issuance.

children.name: Pricing change lead time
children.description: Reduce median lead time for a live pricing or packaging change to under 3 business days.

children.name: Tax coverage on obligated jurisdictions

children.name: Usage billing attach
children.description: Reach 28% usage-based metering adoption among target software accounts.

children.name: Month-end support escalation rate
children.description: Keep month-end critical support escalations below 1.8% of active recurring accounts.

children.name: Revenue export accuracy
children.description: Keep material export or recognition discrepancies below 0.10% of monthly recognized revenue.

productStrategy.vision: Make Stripe the fastest path from monetization idea to trusted recurring revenue operations.

supporting: Pricing change lead time under 3 business days

Failed-payment recovery becomes more measurable and controllable for operators

strategicMilestones: Billing, invoicing, and tax become one launch path for global SaaS teams

customers.name: Platform Payments GM
customers.description: A product or business leader responsible for onboarding sellers, service providers, or creators; moving funds between parties; and monetizing platform payments without creating compliance or operational drag. Success means faster account activation, reliable payouts, and scalable controls for regulated money movement.
careAbout: Onboarding connected accounts quickly without excessive verification failure
Supporting marketplace or platform payouts across countries and seller types
Keeping funds flows, fees, reserves, and reconciliations understandable
Reducing compliance and fraud risk without blocking legitimate platform growth
Launching financial workflows that feel native to the platform experience
winsThem: Configurable connected-account models and onboarding flows
Strong payout, split settlement, and fee collection primitives
Integrated KYC, identity, and risk controls
Clear ledger, balance, and reconciliation views for platform operators
A path into issuing or treasury-style experiences when the platform matures
theirFear: Growing the platform while onboarding friction prevents provider activation
Handling complex funds flows with poor operational visibility or reconciliation gaps
Crossing a compliance threshold before the internal operations model is ready

jobsToBeDone.name: Onboard and activate counterparties at scale
jobsToBeDone.what_it_is: Turn providers, sellers, or creators into verified and transacting connected accounts without heavy manual review.
jobsToBeDone.outcome: Higher activated supply and faster time to first earnings event.

steps.description: Configure onboarding, responsibility boundaries, and dashboard exposure for different partner types.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Connected Account Lifecycle
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Defines account types, capabilities, onboarding states, and lifecycle controls.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: KYC and KYB Verification
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Collects and validates the identity and business data needed to activate accounts.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Identity Verification
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Adds document and identity checks where the platform flow requires stronger assurance.

steps.description: Move from onboarding into first charge, first transfer, or first payout without avoidable operations delay.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Funds Routing and Split Settlement
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Routes incoming funds, fees, reserves, and net transfers correctly across parties.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Global Payouts and Treasury Workflows
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Moves money out to connected accounts on the right schedule and rails.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Eventing and Webhooks
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Notifies platform systems when accounts, payouts, and funds-flow states change.

jobsToBeDone.name: Operate platform money movement safely and profitably
jobsToBeDone.what_it_is: Manage splits, fees, reserves, payouts, disputes, and financial controls without losing track of who owns what money or why.
jobsToBeDone.outcome: Platform payments grow with strong operator trust and fewer manual exceptions.

steps.step: Control funds, fees, and payout timing
steps.description: Run complex platform settlements while keeping the unit economics and operator tools clear.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Funds Routing and Split Settlement
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Calculates platform fees, seller allocations, reserves, and transfer sequencing.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Balances and Internal Ledger
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Maintains money-state traceability across platform and connected-account balances.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Reconciliation and Balance Operations
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Helps platform finance and ops teams close the loop on payouts and exceptions.

steps.step: Protect the platform from fraud and compliance failure
steps.description: Control risky counterparties and money movement without slowing healthy platform supply too much.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Fraud Detection and Adaptive Risk Controls
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Scores risky payment and platform activity in real time.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Disputes and Chargeback Operations
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Handles downstream payment disputes that can affect platform economics.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Capital and Financing Offers
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Lets mature platforms extend financing workflows tied to connected-account performance.

customerJourneyStories.name: Marketplace activation and payout reliability at scale
customerJourneyStories.summary: A platform payments GM needs to activate more supply, move money to counterparties predictably, and keep operator controls strong as the platform expands internationally.

stages.narrative: Platform growth creates pressure to onboard more sellers or providers across additional countries and risk segments.
The GM looks for infrastructure that reduces custom compliance, payout, and funds-flow engineering.
They compare onboarding flexibility, KYC depth, payout reach, ledger clarity, and support for platform monetization.
They launch a connected-account cohort and measure activation, first-earnings speed, and payout exceptions.
Operational confidence grows when account states, balances, disputes, and transfers all fit one coherent model.
Stripe stays embedded when platform expansion, compliance, and money movement remain manageable without a large internal fintech stack.

children.name: Verification completion rate
